# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def convert
    image = params[:image]
    format = params[:format] || 'png'
    size = params[:size] || '100x100'

    unless image.present? && %w[jpg jpeg png].include?(format)
      render json: { error: 'Invalid parameters' }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      return
    end

    input_path = Rails.root.join('tmp', 'input_image.jpg')
    output_path = Rails.root.join('public', "converted_image.#{format}")

    File.open(input_path, 'wb') do |file|
      file.write(image.read)
    end

    system("convert #{input_path} -resize #{size} #{output_path}")

    send_file output_path, type: "image/#{format}", disposition: 'inline'

  ensure
    FileUtils.rm_f input_path if File.exist?(input_path)
    FileUtils.rm_f output_path if File.exist?(output_path)
  end
end
